content.problemStatement:
Recently, Chef has moved to an evil land. In this land, there is only one bank, which offers NNN savings plans (numbered 111 through NNN).

Initially, Chef has 111 coin in his account. He may choose bank plans for as long as he wishes, in any order; each plan may be chosen any number of times. For each valid iii, whenever Chef chooses the iii-th savings plan, the number of coins in his bank account gets multiplied by the interest factor PiP_iPi​ of this plan (whenever he chooses this plan again, the number of coins is multiplied by PiP_iPi​ again).

Since the bank in the evil land is evil, it always stores the number of coins in each account modulo MMM. Strangely, MMM is a prime number.

Chef knows that he will win the lottery if the number of coins in his bank account becomes exactly FFF. The bank does not want Chef to win, so it decided to remove some of the NNN savings plans.

You are the head of the evil bank. Therefore, you need to remove some (possibly none or all) plans in such a way that Chef cannot win the lottery. Find the minimum number of plans you must remove.
constraints: 0≤Pi<M0 \le P_i \lt M0≤Pi​<M for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and FFF.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of plans to remove.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef succeeds when he has 333 coins in his bank account. He can achieve that by choosing plan 111 and then plan 222, since he would get (2⋅4)%5=3(2 \cdot 4) \% 5 = 3(2⋅4)%5=3 coins.
It is enough to delete plan 111. Then, Chef cannot get 333 coins, since he can only multiply the number of coins in his account by 444.
Example case 3: Strangely, Chef succeeds when he has no coins in his bank account. The only way to achieve that is multiplying by 000, so it is enough to delete plan 555.
